canonicalization_execution_2026_04_16.triggered_by: User GO-mode approval (implicit via 'go'); unblocks E-deploy step 1-2 (backup verify + restore smoke)
canonicalization_execution_2026_04_16.canonical_step_rationale: Latest available training step in R2 (r9/step_500..step_3000). Task instruction explicitly defaulted to step_3000 as 'latest is the natural canonical final'. No eval_loss basis found in this audit file favoring step_2000; step_2000 manifest was only sampled for size reference, not selected as canonical.

verification.method: rclone size + rclone ls byte-exact listing compare between src and dst
verification.sha256_manifest: NOT GENERATED — rclone server-side copy uses R2 integrity (S3 ETag/MD5) end-to-end; local SHA256 would require 1.04 GiB egress. Size+count+byte parity verification accepted as sufficient for canonicalization (SHA256 deferred if E-deploy restore smoke requires it).

unblocks: E-deploy step 1 — R2 backup verify against canonical r9/step_final/ key
E-deploy step 2 — restore smoke test (download r9/step_final/ → /tmp/ → size/integrity check)
notes: step_3000 source remains intact (copy, not move). r9/step_500..step_3000 untouched.
Both prefixes now resolve to the same 10-object manifest byte-for-byte.

If user later decides step_2000 is the true eval-loss winner, this canonicalization can be overwritten with `rclone copy --checksum` of step_2000/ over step_final/ (extra step: delete stale bytes first via `rclone purge` or rely on overwrite semantics).
